mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a weakening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts and consumer spending signals a strengthening demand environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over 1 month signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in GDP and consumer spending signals a strengthening demand environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
